name: Stamina Movement
description: A locomotion layer that spends and restores stamina to gate sprinting, climbing, swimming, dodging, or other high-output movement actions.
design_notes: Stamina movement is a resource contract layered over locomotion. Define exactly which movement_state_id spends stamina, when regeneration begins, how exhaustion blocks actions, and whether stamina is restored by resting, items, terrain, or checkpoints. The mechanic should create route and risk decisions without turning basic navigation into a meter-watching chore.
design_purpose: Limit sustained high-output movement without disabling basic navigation.
Create meaningful route planning, escape timing, and recovery decisions.
Connect traversal, survival, combat, and progression through one readable endurance budget.
player_fantasy: The player feels physically committed, pushing their character hard and choosing when to recover before the next demand.

parameters.name: stamina_pool_units
parameters.description: Total endurance budget available for movement actions.

parameters.name: sprint_drain_units_per_second
parameters.description: Drain applied while sprint movement is accepted.

parameters.name: climb_drain_units_per_meter
parameters.description: Drain applied from vertical traversal distance during climbing.

parameters.name: stamina_regen_delay_ms
parameters.description: Delay after last spend before recovery begins.

parameters.name: exhaustion_lockout_ms
parameters.description: Action-gating window after stamina reaches zero.

parameters.name: warning_threshold_units
parameters.description: UI and audio warning threshold before exhaustion.

edge_cases: Stamina can reach zero while the player is airborne, climbing, swimming, or inside an uninterruptible animation.
Pause menus, cutscenes, and dialogue need a timer_pause_policy so drain and recovery do not continue invisibly.
Save/load should restore stamina_resource and exhaustion_state without granting repeated recovery bursts.
Multiplayer clients must not spend stamina locally and then keep unauthorized movement after server correction.
common_bugs: Drain is applied twice because both movement state and animation event spend stamina_resource.
Regeneration starts immediately after every frame with no accepted input due to missing stamina_regen_delay_ms.
Exhaustion blocks UI, camera, or ordinary walking instead of only the intended high-output actions.
Save migration changes max stamina but does not clamp existing profile values.
balancing_notes: Shared stamina across sprint, dodge, block, and climb creates strong tradeoffs but can make one dominant action starve the others.
Regeneration delay matters as much as pool size; long delays create stop-start pacing even with large pools.
Expose enough recovery opportunities that stamina creates planning pressure rather than repeated dead ends.
accessibility_notes: Offer assist settings for reduced drain, longer exhaustion warnings, or less severe lockout in traversal-heavy games.
Do not communicate low stamina only through color or controller vibration; include text, shape, or audio alternatives.

implementation_notes.unity: Keep stamina_resource as data owned by the character, not by individual MonoBehaviours. Movement states request spends with a transaction result, UI reads warning_threshold_units, and FixedUpdate applies accepted locomotion only after stamina gating succeeds.
implementation_notes.godot: Store stamina and exhaustion_state on the player node or Resource. In _physics_process, movement modes request drain and receive accepted or rejected state before velocity changes; save stamina_resource with checkpoint data.
implementation_notes.unreal: Model stamina through an AttributeSet or replicated component. Gameplay Abilities should commit costs before sprint, dodge, or climb activation, while the server validates exhaustion and clients predict only within the allowed movement window.
implementation_notes.web: Use one deterministic stamina ledger in the simulation loop. Apply spend requests before movement integration, record recovery start ticks, and reconcile server-approved stamina_resource when networking is present.

relationships.reason: Movement stamina costs need a stamina stat, regeneration rule, and exhaustion state to resolve consistently.
Stamina movement commonly limits sprint uptime and turns sprint into a resource decision instead of a permanent state.
Readable stamina or recovery indicators help players understand when movement abilities are available again.

example_games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
example_games.note: Uses stamina to gate climbing, gliding, and sprinting.

example_games.title: Dark Souls
example_games.note: Uses stamina to constrain movement, blocking, and attacks.
